Sentences used:
(1) Anna handed a key to Mark.
(2) The train leaves at noon.
(3) When does the train leave?
Knowledge used:
If X has type Y and a situation Z is earlier than a situation W then X has type Y. Why: assumed basic knowledge.
If a situation X is followed by a situation Y then X is earlier than Y. Why: assumed basic knowledge.
Proof steps:
(1) If X happens some Z time some Y and X is a leave event and the train A performs X and X is an activity then [Z, Y] is the answer  [sentence 3]
(2) technical answer unwrap step  [from question]
(3) If X happens some Z time some Y and X is a leave event and the train A performs X and X is an activity then Z is an answer  [from steps 1, 2]
(4) If X has type Y and a situation Z is earlier than a situation W then X has type Y  [background knowledge]
(5) The event act12 is a leave event  [sentence 2]
(6) If the situation W0 is earlier than a situation X then the event act12 is a leave event  [from steps 4, 5]
(7) If a situation X is followed by a situation Y then X is earlier than Y  [background knowledge]
(8) The situation W0 is followed by the situation W1  [sentence 1]
(9) The situation W0 is earlier than the situation W1  [from steps 7, 8]
(10) The event act12 is a leave event  [from steps 6, 9]
(11) If the event act12 happens some Y3 time some X3 and the train A performs the act12 and the act12 is an activity then Y3 is an answer  [from steps 3, 10]
(12) The event act12 is an activity  [sentence 2]
(13) If the event act12 happens some Y time some X and the train A performs the act12 then Y is an answer  [from steps 11, 12]
(14) The train A performs the event act12  [sentence 2]
(15) If the event act12 happens some Y time some X then Y is an answer  [from steps 13, 14]
(16) The event act12 happens at noon  [sentence 2]
